county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1836 from unorganized territory in west-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Grand Rapids, Michigan's second-largest city. Named for James Kent, New York jurist. Major furniture manufacturing center and Dutch immigrant settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Kent County Register of Deeds in Grand Rapids holds land records from 1836 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Grand Rapids Public Library has one of Michigan's finest genealogical collections with city directories (1856+), newspapers, family files, and local history. The Grand Rapids History Center (formerly Grand Rapids Public Museum Archives) holds business records, photographs, and organizational records. Dutch Reformed church records are essential -- Holland and Grand Rapids were major Dutch immigrant destinations (1847+). The Calvin University Heritage Hall archives hold Christian Reformed Church records. Furniture industry employment records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records and church records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Kent County Register of Deeds online access for recorded documents

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1836. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1836. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Kent County from 1840 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Kent County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Dutch Reformed Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Joint Archives of Holland / Calvin University Heritage Hall / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Reformed Church in America (RCA) and Christian Reformed Church (CRC) records from Dutch immigrant congregations. Baptisms, memberships, marriages, and death records. The Joint Archives of Holland (Hope College) and Calvin University Heritage Hall in Grand Rapids hold extensive Dutch Reformed church records. Essential for tracing Dutch immigrant families who settled in western Michigan from 1847.

source_profiles.source_name: Automobile Industry and Labor Records -- Kent County
source_profiles.repository: Walter P. Reuther Library (Wayne State University) / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Automobile manufacturing employment records, union (UAW) records, and industrial documentation for Kent County. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds United Auto Workers records and related labor archives. City directories and employee rosters trace worker families. Essential for 20th-century family research in Michigan's industrial counties.

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1836 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Historical Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Kent County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1861 from Houghton County in the Upper Peninsula. County seat is Eagle River. Named from Ojibwe for 'portage.' Michigan's least populous county. Copper mining heritage at the tip of the Keweenaw Peninsula.
county_metadata.research_tips: Keweenaw County Register of Deeds in Eagle River holds land records from 1861. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. Keweenaw County is Michigan's least populous county at the tip of the Keweenaw Peninsula. Copper mining was the sole industry -- Cliff Mine, Phoenix Mine, and other operations. Michigan Technological University Archives and Copper Country Historical Collections in Houghton hold mining records. Finnish, Cornish, and German miners predominated. Check Houghton County for supplementary records. Finnish American Heritage Center at Finlandia University. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Remote location means many records are held centrally at Archives of Michigan.

source_profiles.source_name: Keweenaw County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1861 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Keweenaw County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1861. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Keweenaw County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1861. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Keweenaw County from 1870 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Keweenaw County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.repository: Michigan Technological University Archives / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Employment rolls, payroll records, accident reports, and company town records from copper mining operations in Keweenaw County. Michigan Tech's Copper Country Historical Collections and the Archives of Michigan hold mining company records. Essential for tracing immigrant mining families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1871 from Mason and Newaygo in west-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Baldwin. Named for its numerous lakes. Home to Idlewild, a historically significant African American resort community.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lake County Register of Deeds in Baldwin holds land records from 1871 organization from Mason and Newaygo counties. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Lake County Historical Society has local materials. Lumber industry drove initial settlement. Lake County has a significant African American community dating from the early 1900s -- Idlewild was a famous African American resort community (1912-1960s) attracting Black families from across the Midwest. Check Archives of Michigan for Idlewild documentation. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Very rural county with limited institutional resources -- state-level repositories are essential.

source_profiles.source_name: Lake County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1871 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records

source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Local historical and genealogical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Lake County churches, township, and family burial grounds. Find A Grave has growing online coverage. Local historical and genealogical societies have published cemetery surveys. Michigan Cemetery Source at the Library of Michigan is a statewide index.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1835 from Oakland and St. Clair in eastern Lower Michigan. County seat is Lapeer. Named from French 'la pierre' (the stone). Agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lapeer County Register of Deeds in Lapeer holds land records from 1835 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from 1835 and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Lapeer County Historical Society has genealogical collections. Early settlers came from New York and New England. Lapeer County was agricultural with some milling. The county was formed from Oakland and St. Clair. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has territorial-period records. The Marguerite deAngeli Library in Lapeer has local history materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Lapeer County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1863 from Grand Traverse County in northwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Leland. Named from a Schoolcraft coinage. Scenic peninsula county on Lake Michigan with cherry orchards and wineries.
county_metadata.research_tips: Leelanau County Register of Deeds in Leland holds land records from 1863 when organized from Grand Traverse. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Leelanau Historical Society Museum has local genealogical materials. The Grand Traverse Band of Ottawa and Chippewa Indians has significant presence in Leelanau County -- tribal enrollment records at tribal offices and NARA. Cherry farming heritage. Scandinavian and Bohemian immigrant communities settled in the area. Leland was a commercial fishing port. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Check Grand Traverse County for pre-1863 records. BLM GLO land patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Leelanau County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1863 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1863. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Leelanau County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa/Odawa Tribal Records -- Leelanau County Area
source_profiles.repository: NARA (Great Lakes Agency) / Tribal Government Offices / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Treaty records, tribal census rolls (annuity rolls), allotment records, and agency correspondence for Ottawa/Odawa communities in Leelanau County. NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1826 from Monroe County in southeast Lower Michigan. County seat is Adrian. Named from a Shawnee word meaning 'man.' One of Michigan's earliest organized counties. Underground Railroad heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lenawee County Register of Deeds in Adrian holds land records from 1826 -- one of Michigan's earliest organized counties. County Clerk maintains marriage records from the 1820s. Probate Court has estate files. The Lenawee County Historical Museum in Adrian has genealogical collections. Adrian College (founded 1859, Methodist) archives hold local materials. Lenawee County was heavily involved in the Underground Railroad -- the Raisin Valley Anti-Slavery Society and Laura Haviland's work are documented. Quaker and Methodist church records are essential for early settlers. Irish settlers in Cambridge Township. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. The Archives of Michigan has early territorial records for Lenawee.

source_profiles.source_name: Lenawee County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1826 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Lenawee County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1826. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1826. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records -- Lenawee County
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Burton Historical Collection (Detroit Public Library)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan was a free state, but the Underground Railroad was active throughout southern Michigan with routes to Canada. Lenawee County records include freedom seekers' documentation, abolitionist society records, African American church records, and community histories. The Burton Historical Collection and Archives of Michigan hold related materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Lenawee County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1826 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Lenawee County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1836 from Shiawassee and Washtenaw in southeast Lower Michigan. County seat is Howell. Named for Edward Livingston, US Secretary of State. Agricultural county, later suburban growth.
county_metadata.research_tips: Livingston County Register of Deeds in Howell holds land records from 1836 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from 1836 and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Livingston County Genealogical Society has compiled family files and cemetery records. Early settlers came from New York (the county is named for Edward Livingston). Agricultural county with some milling. Strong Presbyterian, Methodist, and Congregational church records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has supplementary records. Brighton and Howell newspapers archive local events.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1836 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1887 from Chippewa and Mackinac in the eastern Upper Peninsula. County seat is Newberry. Named for Cyrus G. Luce, Michigan governor. Lumber heritage; home to Tahquamenon Falls.
county_metadata.research_tips: Luce County Register of Deeds in Newberry holds land records from 1887 organization from Chippewa and Mackinac counties. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Luce County Historical Museum in Newberry has local materials. Lumber industry was the primary economic driver. The Tahquamenon Falls area attracted early tourism. Finnish and French-Canadian settlers formed the primary population. Check Chippewa and Mackinac counties for pre-1887 records. Michigan Technological University Archives has UP lumber records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Remote UP county with limited local resources -- Archives of Michigan is essential.

source_profiles.source_name: Luce County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1887 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1887. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Luce County from 1890 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Luce County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

Local historical and genealogical societies have published cemetery surveys. Michigan Cemetery Source at the Library of Michigan is a statewide index.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1818 as one of Michigan Territory's original counties from Wayne County. County seat is St. Ignace. Named from Ojibwe 'Michilimackinac' (Great Turtle). Historic fur trade and military center. Mackinac Island is a National Historic Landmark.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mackinac County Register of Deeds in St. Ignace holds land records from 1818, making it one of Michigan Territory's original counties. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Mackinac Island State Park Commission Archives has fur trade, military, and early settlement records. Fort Mackinac (British 1780, American 1796) records at NARA. The Mackinac Island community has unique records -- John Jacob Astor's American Fur Company records at the Bentley Historical Library (University of Michigan). French-Canadian, Metis, and Odawa (Ottawa) families intermarried for generations. Catholic parish records (Ste. Anne's) from the 1700s. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records.

source_profiles.source_name: Mackinac County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Mackinac County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1818. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1818. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Mackinac County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Detroit Archives / Archives of Michigan / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catholic parish records including baptisms, marriages, and burials. Ste. Anne de Michilimackinac records document fur trade families. French-Canadian families from the fur trade era are well documented in Catholic registers. FamilySearch has microfilmed many Michigan Catholic parish records.

source_profiles.repository: NARA (Great Lakes Agency) / Tribal Government Offices / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Treaty records, tribal census rolls (annuity rolls), allotment records, and agency correspondence for Ojibwe/Chippewa communities in Mackinac County. NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1818 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1818 as one of Michigan Territory's original counties from Wayne County. County seat is Mt. Clemens. Named for General Alexander Macomb. Suburban county north of Detroit.
county_metadata.research_tips: Macomb County Register of Deeds in Mt. Clemens holds land records from 1818 (original Michigan Territory county). County Clerk maintains marriage records from the 1820s and court records. Probate Court has estate files from early territorial period. The Macomb County Historical Society has genealogical collections. The Clinton-Macomb Public Library has local history resources. German immigrants were numerous in Macomb County. Mt. Clemens was a famous mineral bath resort town (late 1800s). Selfridge Air National Guard Base records. Automobile industry workers from nearby Detroit settled in Macomb County. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records and some church records. BLM GLO land patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Macomb County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.
